rationale: Review 1 identifies a real internal tension (title says 'elementary abelian' while body defers '(ℤ/2)ⁿ'), but its diagnosis is mathematically questionable: elementary abelian of exponent 2 is definitionally equivalent to abelian+exponent-2, so the title does not overclaim. Acting on Review 1's proposed title change would weaken a defensibly accurate title without fixing the body's ambiguous framing; acting on Review 2 leaves things unchanged. Neither resulting PR is meaningfully better.
